methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $690M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Bethlehem Steel Corp is a Growth-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $690M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 peer companies in Robotics & Manufacturing (1 public, 8 private), including firms such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and forms the sole quantitative anchor for this analysis. Using total capital raised of $690M as a proxy for recent valuation and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $828M. A Peer-Median approach was not executable; while 7 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making median multiples unreliable without introducing undue selection bias.

The triangulated range spans $704M (low), $828M (median), and $952M (high), derived conservatively from the single applicable method with symmetric variance bands. Key uncertainties center on the absence of narrow-peer financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA, growth rates) necessary to apply revenue or earnings multiples, and the early-stage nature of the private peer set. A fuller valuation would require access to recent round pricing, revenue run-rate, and customer traction data for comparable Growth-stage robotics companies.
